title: How to proceed with combination meter self-diagnosis

blocks.text: How to proceed with combination meter self-diagnosis
Combination meters have the ability to self-diagnose the circuits in the meter as described below.

items.text: Indicator and warning light drive circuits
Speed meter drive circuit, tachometer drive circuit
Multiplex communication between units via Body Controller Area Network (B-CAN), Fast Controller Area Network (F-CAN) and other communication lines (LIN)

blocks.text: Before performing self-diagnosis of the combination meter, check for blown or connected No. A14 (10A) fuse in the relay box and No. D4 (7.5A) and No. D1 (7.5A) fuses in the sub-fuse/relay box.
Select "Meter" under "Body Electrical" in HDS and select "Function Test". Combination Meter Self-Diagnostic Function" in the "Function Test".
Before performing self-diagnosis of the combination meter, check for blown or connected No. A14 (10A) fuse in the relay box and No. D4 (7.5A) and No. D1 (7.5A) fuses in the sub-fuse/relay box.
5. Press the select/reset knob three times within 5 seconds to enter self-diagnostic mode.

items.text: The illumination control operates normally even in self-diagnostic mode.
Display switching and trip reset cannot be performed with the select/reset knob during self-diagnostic mode.
If there is a vehicle speed input of 2 km/h or more during the self-diagnostic mode, the self-diagnostic mode is canceled and the system returns to the normal mode.

blocks.text: During self-diagnostic mode, the indicator, warning light and shift position indicator flash continuously at intervals of 1 second cycle.
During self-diagnostic mode, the ambient meter changes color in the order of white, white-green, and green.
Immediately after shifting to self-diagnostic mode, all segments of the information display blink five times in synchronization with the blinking of indicator/warning lights.
Immediately after shifting to self-diagnostic mode, the indicator/warning light blows intermittently 5 times in synchronization with the indicator/warning light blinking.
Immediately after shifting to the self-diagnostic mode, the display of each meter is driven from the minimum position to the maximum position and then back to the minimum position.

items.text: After the buzzer sounds, the operation is repeated each time the Select/Reset knob is pressed after each meter display returns to the minimum position.
No signal is accepted by pressing the select/reset knob until each meter display returns to the minimum position.

blocks.text: After diagnosing the segment of the information display, perform diagnosis of multiplex communication.

items.text: If there is no abnormality in multiplex communication, the information display turns on all lights.

If there is an error in multiplex communication, Error lights up on the information display: check the F-CAN communication line when Error1 is displayed, the B-CAN communication line when Error2 is displayed, other communication lines when Error3 is displayed, and the F-CAN communication line and other communication lines when Error12 is displayed. -CAN and B-CAN communication lines when Error12 is displayed, B-CAN communication lines when Error123 is displayed, and F-CAN communication lines and other communication lines when Error123 is displayed. If there is an abnormality in more than one communication line at the same time, the corresponding code is displayed after Error.

blocks.text: After the buzzer drive circuit check is completed, the VSA OFF switch, parking brake switch, select/reset knob, ECON mode switch, front defroster switch, cruise control combination switch (CRUISE/CANSEL/SET-/RES+), and city While each switch of the brake active system OFF switch is ON, the buzzer will sound continuously. Switches that were ON before checking the buzzer drive circuit must be turned OFF and then ON to accept the signal.

The combination meter sends a customization reset signal to each unit via the B-CAN communication line to reset the user-customized items to the factory defaults. Self-diagnosis continues during the Customize Reset operation.
2. Holding the Select/Reset knob down for more than 10 seconds causes the buzzer to sound for 10 seconds and resets the customization data that can be operated on the meter.
Ignition OFF (LOCK) mode. Or vehicle speed input of 2 km/h or more will deactivate it.
